verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone, but it is well-documented to be displayed on the outer leaflet of stressed and tumor cells (membrane Hsp70, mHsp70) via association with phosphatidylserine / Bag-4 and lipid rafts. This tumor-specific surface display is the basis of clinical programs (e.g. cmHsp70.1 antibody, NK-cell therapies targeting membrane Hsp70). Surface exposure is also induced during heat shock, oxidative stress, and immunogenic cell death. The protein body therefore reaches the outer leaflet only under stress/oncogenic states, fitting cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
